1	---
2	name: lean-scaffolder
3	description: "Structural file setup. Reads blueprints, strategy, and PROGRESS to generate Lean declarations with `sorry` bodies and rich `/- Planner strategy: ... -/` implementation comments."
4	write_domain: "*.lean"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch this agent when you have "Medium content" implementation hints (e.g. typeclass strategies, verified Mathlib lemma paths) that the prover needs.
10	  - The scaffolder injects exact Lean signatures and your hints into a block comment directly above the `sorry`.
11	  - It can verify Mathlib functions using Lean search tools if needed.
12	---
13	
14	# Lean Scaffolder Subagent
15	
16	Your job is to bridge the gap between the plan agent's strategy and the prover's execution. You inject Lean scaffolding and implementation hints into `.lean` files.
17	
18	## Your Tasks
19	
20	1. **Read Context:**
21	   - Read the planner's directive, `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, and the relevant blueprint chapter to understand the math and the implementation plan.
22	2. **Scaffold Lean Declarations:**
23	   - Create the exact Lean signatures requested by the planner.
24	   - Leave the bodies as `sorry`. Do NOT attempt to prove the theorems.
25	3. **Inject Strategy Comments:**
26	   - Place the planner's implementation ideas directly into the `.lean` file.
27	   - Use comments like `/- Blueprint note: ... -/` or `/- Planner strategy: ... -/` right above the target declaration.
28	   - Translate the high-level strategy into actionable advice for the prover (e.g., "split into these two cases", "use the verified mathlib lemma X").
29	4. **Verify Lean Names:**
30	   - You MUST NOT invent or hallucinate Lean names.
31	   - If the planner suggests a name, or if you are translating a mathematical concept, use `lean_leansearch` or `lean_loogle` to verify the existence of the Lean function/lemma.
32	   - If a name is unknown, tag it clearly or leave it out.
33	5. **Keep the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence:**
34	   - Every declaration you scaffold should correspond to a blueprint entry (the directive normally names the chapter and labels — the planner creates the entries before or alongside your dispatch). Lean never exists without tex.
35	   - You cannot write `.tex` (outside your write-domain): when the directive asks you to scaffold a declaration with NO corresponding blueprint entry, scaffold it anyway but list it prominently in your report under "Uncovered declarations" — declaration name, file, and its dependencies — so the planner adds the entry the same iteration.
36	
37	Return your outcome and the path to your report.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
162

1	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic
2	import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
3	import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
4	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
5	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal
6	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization
8	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal
9	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.PreservesSheafification
10	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Adjunction
11	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Symmetric
13	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
14	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.ZModuleEquivalence
15	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf
16	
17	/-!
18	# Section graded ring infrastructure, Layer 1: tensor powers of a sheaf of modules
19	
20	This file builds the Mathlib-absent infrastructure of
21	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`, Layer 1
22	(`sec:sgr_tensor_powers`): the tensor product, tensor powers, and twists of
23	sheaves of modules over a scheme `X`, together with the unitor and braiding
24	isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
25	
26	The category `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` of sheaves of modules
27	over a scheme carries **no** monoidal structure in Mathlib (the structure sheaf
28	varies the base ring over opens).  Mathlib *does* supply:
29	
30	* the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of **presheaves** of modules
31	  `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
32	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal`), and
33	* the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`
34	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification`).
35	
36	We therefore build the tensor product of sheaves of modules as the sheafification
37	of the objectwise (presheaf) tensor product, following
38	[Stacks, Tag 01CA].
39	
40	## Main definitions
41	
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification` — the scheme-level
43	  sheafification functor `X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules`.
44	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) —
45	  `F ⊗ G := (F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)^#`.
46	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow` (`def:sheafTensorPow`) —
47	  the `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules.
48	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`) —
49	  the `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}`.
50	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCounitIso` — the reflective
51	  counit iso `(F.toPresheaf)^# ≅ F`.
52	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjUnitIso`,
53	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjRightUnitor`,
54	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorBraiding` — the left/right unitor and
55	  braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
56	
57	The comparison isomorphism `L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}`
58	(`lem:sheafTensorPow_add`) is **deferred**: see the handoff note before the end
59	of the file for the single missing ingredient (strong-monoidality of the module
60	sheafification functor) and the launching pad assembled here.
61	-/
62	
63	universe u
64	
65	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory Limits
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
68	
69	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
70	
71	/-- The scheme-level sheafification functor, sending a presheaf of modules over a
72	scheme `X` to its associated sheaf of modules `X.Modules`.  It is the
73	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification` functor for the identity morphism of the
74	underlying presheaf of rings (which is locally bijective).  Non-private because it
75	appears in the statement of `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
76	noncomputable def sheafification : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules :=
77	  PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
78	
79	/-- The category `X.PresheafOfModules` of presheaves of modules over a scheme,
80	presented in the exact form `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`
81	for which Mathlib equips it with a symmetric monoidal structure.  This is
82	*definitionally* `X.PresheafOfModules` (since
83	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`), so a term of
84	either type is accepted for the other. -/
85	private abbrev MonoidalPresheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
86	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
87	
88	/-- The tensor product of two sheaves of modules over a scheme, defined as the
89	sheafification of the objectwise tensor product presheaf
90	(Mathlib `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  See [Stacks, Tag 01CA]
91	(`def:sheafTensorObj`). -/
92	noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
93	  sheafification.obj
94	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
95	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
96	
97	/-- The structure sheaf as a sheaf of modules over itself: the unit object of the
98	tensor product, i.e. the zeroth tensor power `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X`
99	(`def:unitModule`, backed by `lem:moduleUnit_mathlib`).  Public: the SNAP graded
100	assembly (`sectionsMul_assoc_unit`, `lem:sectionMul_coherent`) states unitality
101	against this object. -/
102	noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
103	  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf
104	
105	/-- The `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules over a scheme, defined
106	by recursion: `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X` (the unit module) and
107	`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/
108	noncomputable def tensorPow (L : X.Modules) : ℕ → X.Modules
109	  | 0 => unitModule X
110	  | (m + 1) => tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L
111	
112	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_zero (L : X.Modules) : tensorPow L 0 = unitModule X := rfl
113	
114	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_succ (L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) :
115	    tensorPow L (m + 1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L := rfl
116	
117	/-- The `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules `F` by the `m`-th
118	tensor power of a line bundle `L` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`).  This is the
119	degree-`m` carrier of the section graded module. -/
120	noncomputable def moduleTensorPow (F L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) : X.Modules :=
121	  tensorObj F (tensorPow L m)
122	
123	@[simp] private lemma moduleTensorPow_zero (F L : X.Modules) :
124	    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl
125	
126	/-! ### Unitor and braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product
127	
128	These are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
129	that descend through sheafification from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
130	using only *functoriality* of `sheafification` (and, for the unitors, the
131	reflective counit iso) — no strong-monoidality of `sheafification` is needed, so
132	they are axiom-clean.  They are the launching pad for `tensorPowAdd`. -/
133	
134	/-- The counit isomorphism of the module sheafification adjunction: sheafifying
135	the underlying presheaf of a sheaf of modules returns the sheaf itself.  This is
136	an isomorphism because the counit of `sheafification ⊣ toPresheafOfModules` is
137	invertible (the right adjoint `SheafOfModules.forget` is fully faithful).  It is
138	the launching pad for the left-unitor base case of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
139	private noncomputable def sheafificationCounitIso (G : X.Modules) :
140	    sheafification.obj ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ≅ G :=
141	  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
142	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).counit).app G
143	
144	/-- The left-unitor isomorphism `unitModule X ⊗ G ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
145	product: the presheaf left unitor `λ_` descended through sheafification, composed
146	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  This is the base case (`m = 0`) of
147	`tensorPowAdd`.  Axiom-clean. -/
148	private noncomputable def tensorObjUnitIso (G : X.Modules) :
149	    tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G :=
150	  sheafification.mapIso
151	      (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
152	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
153	    sheafificationCounitIso G
154	
155	/-- The right-unitor isomorphism `G ⊗ unitModule X ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
156	product: the presheaf right unitor `ρ_` descended through sheafification, composed
157	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  Axiom-clean (no monoidal structure
158	on `X.Modules` is required). -/
159	private noncomputable def tensorObjRightUnitor (G : X.Modules) :
160	    tensorObj G (unitModule X) ≅ G :=
161	  sheafification.mapIso
162	      (MonoidalCategory.rightUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
163	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
164	    sheafificationCounitIso G
165	
166	/-- The braiding isomorphism `F ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ F` of the sheaf tensor product,
167	descended through sheafification from the symmetric braiding on
168	`X.PresheafOfModules` (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  Axiom-clean: the
169	braiding is pure sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf-level braiding, so
170	no monoidal structure on `X.Modules` is required.  This is the symmetry used in
171	the inductive step of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
172	private noncomputable def tensorBraiding (F G : X.Modules) :
173	    tensorObj F G ≅ tensorObj G F :=
174	  sheafification.mapIso
175	    (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
176	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
177	
178	/-! ### Lax-monoidal global sections: the section multiplication
179	
180	The global-sections functor `Γ(X, -)` is only *lax* monoidal: a pair of global
181	sections does not commute with sheafification, so the multiplication is a map,
182	not an isomorphism.  It is nonetheless `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)`-linear and is the data the
183	section graded ring is built from. -/
184	
185	/-- The **section multiplication** (`def:sectionMul`), the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear map
186	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G) → Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.
187	
188	Its domain `(F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)(X)` is, by the objectwise formula of
189	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-module
190	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G)` of elementary tensors of global sections; a pair
191	`(σ, τ)` is sent to `σ ⊗ τ`.  Postcomposing with the global-sections component of
192	the sheafification unit `η : P → P^#` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) lands in
193	`Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.  As a morphism in `ModuleCat (Γ(X,𝒪_X))` it is automatically
194	`Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear; this records that linearity.  Axiom-clean: it is pure
195	sheafification-unit naturality, requiring no monoidal structure on `X.Modules`. -/
196	noncomputable def sectionsMul (F G : X.Modules) :
197	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
198	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶
199	      (tensorObj F G).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
200	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
201	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
202	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))).app (Opposite.op ⊤)
203	
204	/-! ### The strong-monoidality comparison `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`
205	
206	Following `analogies/snap-route.md` (Analogue 1) and the blueprint proof of
207	`lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: module sheafification is the
208	localization functor at the class `W := J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)` of
209	morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying abelian-presheaf morphism is a
210	local isomorphism (a `J.W` for the opens topology `J` on `X`). -/
211	
212	/-- The Grothendieck topology on the opens of the scheme `X`. -/
213	private abbrev opensTopology (X : Scheme.{u}) : GrothendieckTopology (TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :=
214	  Opens.grothendieckTopology (X : TopCat)
215	
216	open MorphismProperty in
217	/-- **Localization criterion for module sheafification.**  The scheme-level module
218	sheafification `sheafification.map f` is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules iff the
219	underlying abelian-presheaf morphism `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map f` lies in
220	the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology (i.e. is a local isomorphism of
221	abelian-group presheaves).  This is the reduction step of
222	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: it turns the strong-monoidality comparison
223	into a purely abelian local-isomorphism statement.  Project-local: it specialises
224	`_root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` to the
225	identity morphism of `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`. -/
226	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_iff {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ⟶ Q) :
227	    IsIso (sheafification.map f) ↔
228	      (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map f) := by
229	  have e := _root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms
230	      (J := opensTopology X) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
231	  constructor
232	  · intro h
233	    have h' : ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)).inverseImage
234	        (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj))) f := h
235	    rw [← e] at h'
236	    exact h'
237	  · intro h
238	    have h' : (((opensTopology X).W).inverseImage
239	        (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) f := h
240	    rw [e] at h'
241	    exact h'
242	
243	/-- **The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism.**  The underlying
244	abelian-presheaf morphism of the module sheafification unit `η_P : P ⟶ P^#` is
245	*definitionally* the abelian sheafification unit `toSheafify J P.presheaf`
246	(`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`), which lies
247	in the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology by
248	`GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify`.  Project-local: this is the `η_P ∈ J.W`
249	ingredient of the abelian-`J.W`-monoidality transfer underlying
250	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
251	lemma localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
252	    (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
253	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) := by
254	  rw [PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
255	  exact (opensTopology X).W_toSheafify _
256	
257	/-- **Sheafification inverts the localization unit.**  `sheafification.map η_P` is an
258	isomorphism of sheaves of modules (the reflective-localization unit becomes invertible
259	after sheafifying).  Obtained by feeding `localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit` through the
260	localization criterion `isIso_sheafification_map_iff`.  Project-local: the `m = 0`
261	launching pad and the un-whiskered special case of
262	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
263	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
264	    IsIso (sheafification.map
265	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) :=
266	  (isIso_sheafification_map_iff _).mpr (localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit P)
267	
268	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — relative tensor product as a coequalizer
269	
270	This section builds the **objectwise** content of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`
271	(`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`): over a commutative ring `S` and `S`-modules `M, N`,
272	the relative tensor product `M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer, *in the category of abelian
273	groups*, of the two `S`-action maps
274	
275	  `M ⊗[ℤ] S ⊗[ℤ] N  ⇉  M ⊗[ℤ] N`,    `m ⊗ s ⊗ n ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`  /  `m ⊗ (s • n)`.
276	
277	This is the Mathlib-absent brick on which the strong-monoidality comparison
278	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` rests: the underlying abelian presheaf of the
279	presheaf-level relative tensor `P ⊗_p Q` is, objectwise, exactly this coequalizer.  The
280	universal property is the abelian-group universal property of the relative tensor product,
281	packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`.  Everything here is axiom-clean.
282	
283	The promotion of this objectwise colimit to the presheaf category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrp`
284	(where colimits are computed objectwise) and the identification of the whiskered unit
285	`η_P ▷ Q` with the induced map of coequalizers are the next steps; see the handoff note. -/
286	
287	namespace RelativeTensorCoequalizer
288	
289	open TensorProduct
290	
291	variable (S : Type u) [CommRing S] (M N : Type u)
292	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
293	
294	/-- The `S`-action map `S ⊗[ℤ] N → N`, `s ⊗ n ↦ s • n`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
295	noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
296	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
297	    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
298	    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
299	
300	/-- The `S`-action map `M ⊗[ℤ] S → M`, `m ⊗ s ↦ s • m`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
301	noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
302	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
303	    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
304	    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
305	
306	/-- Right action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`. -/
307	noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
308	  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
309	
310	/-- Left action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`. -/
311	noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
312	  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp
313	    (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
314	
315	omit [Module S M] in
316	@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
317	    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
318	
319	omit [Module S N] in
320	@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
321	    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
322	
323	/-- The canonical projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N`, `m ⊗ n ↦ m ⊗ n`, as a `ℤ`-linear
324	map.  It is the cofork map exhibiting `M ⊗[S] N` as the coequalizer. -/
325	noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
326	  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
327	    { toFun := fun m =>
328	        (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
329	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
330	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp }
331	    (fun r m n => by simp)).toIntLinearMap
332	
333	@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
334	
335	/-- The projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` is surjective (it is the canonical
336	quotient map onto the relative tensor). -/
337	lemma projL_surjective : Function.Surjective (projL S M N) := by
338	  intro y
339	  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
340	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
341	  | tmul m n => exact ⟨m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n, projL_tmul S M N m n⟩
342	  | add a b ha hb =>
343	    obtain ⟨pa, rfl⟩ := ha; obtain ⟨pb, rfl⟩ := hb; exact ⟨pa + pb, map_add _ _ _⟩
344	
345	/-- The two action maps become equal after the projection: this is the cofork
346	coequalizing condition, established at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps. -/
347	lemma projL_comp_act :
348	    (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
349	  apply TensorProduct.ext'; intro m x
350	  induction x with
351	  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
352	  | tmul s n =>
353	    change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
354	      = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
355	    rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, ← TensorProduct.smul_tmul',
356	      TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
357	  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
358	
359	/-- Left action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
360	noncomputable def aL :
361	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
362	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
363	/-- Right action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
364	noncomputable def aR :
365	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
366	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
367	/-- The projection as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
368	noncomputable def piMor :
369	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
370	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
371	
372	@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
373	
374	instance piMor_epi : Epi (piMor S M N) :=
375	  ConcreteCategory.epi_of_surjective (piMor S M N) (projL_surjective S M N)
376	
377	/-- The projection coequalizes the two action maps (as morphisms of abelian groups). -/
378	lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
379	  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
380	
381	/-- The cofork `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` of abelian groups. -/
382	noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
383	  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
384	
385	/-- The descent map out of `M ⊗[S] N` induced by a cofork `s`: a pair of global
386	sections balanced under the `S`-action factors through the relative tensor.  This
387	is the universal property packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. -/
388	noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
389	    (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
390	  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
391	    { toFun := fun m =>
392	        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
393	          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
394	          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
395	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
396	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
397	    (fun a m n => by
398	      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
399	      have key :=
400	        congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) s.condition
401	      simpa using key)
402	
403	@[simp] lemma descHom_tmul (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) (m : M) (n : N) :
404	    descHom S M N s (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) := rfl
405	
406	/-- The descent map as a morphism of abelian groups out of the cofork apex. -/
407	noncomputable def descMor (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
408	    (cofork S M N).pt ⟶ s.pt :=
409	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (descHom S M N s)
410	
411	/-- The descent map factors the cofork's projection: `π ≫ descMor s = s.π`. -/
412	lemma descFac (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
413	    (cofork S M N).π ≫ descMor S M N s = s.π := by
414	  ext x
415	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
416	  | zero => simp
417	  | tmul m n =>
418	    change descHom S M N s (projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
419	    rw [projL_tmul, descHom_tmul]
420	  | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
421	
422	/-- **`M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer**, in the category of abelian groups, of the two
423	`S`-action maps `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N`.  This is the objectwise content of
424	`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`; uniqueness uses that the projection `piMor` is an
425	epimorphism.  Axiom-clean. -/
426	noncomputable def isColimitCofork : Limits.IsColimit (cofork S M N) :=
427	  Limits.Cofork.IsColimit.mk _ (descMor S M N) (descFac S M N)
428	    (fun s _ hf => (cancel_epi (piMor S M N)).mp (hf.trans (descFac S M N s).symm))
429	
430	end RelativeTensorCoequalizer
431	
432	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — presheaf promotion of the coequalizer (Step 1)
433	
434	The objectwise coequalizer `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` exhibits, for a fixed
435	open `U`, the relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{R(U)} Γ(U,Q)` as a coequalizer of the two
436	`R(U)`-action maps on `Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ R(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q) ⇉ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)`.  To promote this
437	to the functor category `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` (where colimits are computed objectwise, via
438	`CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits`) one first needs the two **domain
439	presheaves of the cofork as honest functors**, whose restriction maps are the `ℤ`-tensors of
440	the underlying restriction maps.  This section builds the first of those two functors
441	(`relTensorDomainPresheaf`, the `Γ(-,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(-,Q)` presheaf); it is the concrete Step-1 brick
442	of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` (`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`).
443	
444	See the handoff note at the end of the file for the verified recipe for the remaining pieces
445	(triple-tensor presheaf, the natural action/projection transformations, the colimit lift, and
446	the apex identification) and the heartbeat/coercion friction points that must be budgeted. -/
447	
448	open scoped TensorProduct
449	
450	/-- Restriction map for a presheaf of modules with syntactic `↥(P.obj U)` carriers.
451	The underlying function is `(P.presheaf.map f).hom`; the type annotation forces the
452	domain/codomain to print as `↥(P.obj U)` / `↥(P.obj V)` (not `↥((P.presheaf).obj U)`,
453	which are rfl-defeq but syntactically distinct).  The syntactic agreement is the
454	load-bearing ingredient for `TensorProduct.map_tmul` unification in
455	`relTensorActL.naturality` / `relTensorActR.naturality`. -/
456	private noncomputable def objRestrict (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
457	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
458	    ↥(P.obj U) →ₗ[ℤ] ↥(P.obj V) :=
459	  (show ↥(P.obj U) →+ ↥(P.obj V) from
460	    { toFun := (P.presheaf.map f).hom
461	      map_zero' := map_zero (P.presheaf.map f).hom
462	      map_add' := map_add (P.presheaf.map f).hom }).toIntLinearMap
463	
464	@[simp] private lemma objRestrict_apply (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
465	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (x : ↥(P.obj U)) :
466	    objRestrict P f x = (P.presheaf.map f).hom x := rfl
467	
468	/-- Identity law for the syntactic-carrier restriction: `objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = id`. -/
469	private lemma objRestrict_id (P : X.PresheafOfModules) (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
470	    objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = LinearMap.id := by
471	  ext x
472	  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
473	    AddMonoidHom.id_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
474	
475	/-- Composition law for the syntactic-carrier restriction:
476	`objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g) ∘ (objRestrict P f)`. -/
477	private lemma objRestrict_comp (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
478	    {U V W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (g : V ⟶ W) :
479	    objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g).comp (objRestrict P f) := by
480	  ext x
481	  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp,
482	    AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]
483	
484	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)` of two presheaves of modules
485	over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the `ℤ`-tensors of the
486	two underlying restriction maps.  This is the codomain (apex-adjacent) presheaf of the cofork
487	in the presheaf promotion of `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork`; it is the concrete
488	Step-1 brick of the presheaf-level coequalizer iso `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`
489	(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).  Project-local: no objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of
490	abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib (`AddCommGrpCat` carries no monoidal
491	structure in the current pin). -/
492	noncomputable def relTensorDomainPresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
493	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
494	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U)
495	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
496	    (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).toAddMonoidHom
497	  map_id U := by
498	    ext x
499	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
500	    | zero => simp
501	    | tmul m n =>
502	      simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
503	        objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
504	        AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
505	    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
506	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
507	    ext x
508	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
509	    | zero => simp
510	    | tmul m n =>
511	      simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
512	        objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp,
513	        AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
514	    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
515	
516	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor triple presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ (𝒪_X(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q))` of two
517	presheaves of modules over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the
518	`ℤ`-tensors of the underlying restriction maps (the middle factor restricting via the ring
519	restriction map of `𝒪_X`).  This is the **domain** row of the relative-tensor coequalizer
520	presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`); objectwise it is the triple tensor on which
521	the two `R(U)`-action maps `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`/`actRmap` act.  Project-local: no
522	objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib. -/
523	noncomputable def relTensorTriplePresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
524	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
525	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] (X.sheaf.obj.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U))
526	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
527	    (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
528	      (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
529	        (objRestrict Q f))).toAddMonoidHom
530	  map_id U := by
531	    have hR : (X.sheaf.obj.map (𝟙 U)).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap =
532	        LinearMap.id (R := ℤ) (M := ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) := by
533	      ext s
534	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, CommRingCat.hom_id, RingHom.toAddMonoidHom_eq_coe,
535	        AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
536	      rfl
537	    rw [objRestrict_id P U, objRestrict_id Q U, hR, TensorProduct.map_id, TensorProduct.map_id]
538	    rfl
539	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
540	    have hR : (X.sheaf.obj.map (f ≫ g)).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap =
541	        ((X.sheaf.obj.map g).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap).comp
542	          ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap) := by
543	      ext s
544	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
545	        RingHom.toAddMonoidHom_eq_coe, AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap, LinearMap.coe_comp,
546	        Function.comp_apply]
547	      rfl
548	    rw [objRestrict_comp P f g, objRestrict_comp Q f g, hR, TensorProduct.map_comp,
549	      TensorProduct.map_comp]
550	    rfl
551	
552	/-- The **left-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
553	(`def:relTensorActL`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
554	component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map
555	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
556	action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,P)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality in `U` is the
557	compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps, checked on elementary tensors
558	by `⊗`-induction (the single fact `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged to the abelian
559	restriction by `objRestrict_apply`). -/
560	noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
561	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
562	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
563	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
564	  naturality {U V} f := by
565	    -- The underlying ℤ-linear naturality square, proven by `⊗`-induction.  The single
566	    -- mathematical fact is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` (semilinearity of the restriction).
567	    have key :
568	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
569	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
570	              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
571	                (objRestrict Q f)))
572	          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
573	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
574	                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
575	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
576	      intro m y
577	      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
578	      | zero => simp
579	      | tmul s n =>
580	        change ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict P f m)
581	              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f n
582	            = objRestrict P f (s • m) ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f n
583	        congr 1
584	        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
585	        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul P f s m).symm
586	      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
587	    -- Transport the linear-map square to the categorical naturality square in `Ab`.
588	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
589	    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
590	    ext z
591	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
592	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
593	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz
594	
595	/-- The **right-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
596	(`def:relTensorActR`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
597	component at `U` is the objectwise right-action map
598	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
599	action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,Q)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`.  Naturality is the
600	compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
601	on `Q`), checked on elementary tensors by `⊗`-induction. -/
602	noncomputable def relTensorActR (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
603	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
604	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
605	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
606	  naturality {U V} f := by
607	    have key :
608	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
609	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
610	              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
611	                (objRestrict Q f)))
612	          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
613	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
614	                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
615	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
616	      intro m y
617	      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
618	      | zero => simp
619	      | tmul s n =>
620	        change objRestrict P f m
621	              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict Q f n)
622	            = objRestrict P f m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f (s • n)
623	        congr 1
624	        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
625	        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul Q f s n).symm
626	      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
627	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
628	    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
629	    ext z
630	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
631	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
632	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz
633	
634	/-- The **projection** natural transformation (`relTensorProj`):
635	`relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q ⟶ (toPresheaf).obj (P ⊗_p Q)`, whose component at `U` is the
636	canonical quotient `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL` from the objectwise `ℤ`-tensor onto the
637	relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{𝒪_X(U)} Γ(U,Q)` (the apex of the cofork, identified with the value of
638	the presheaf monoidal tensor by `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj`).  This is the cofork
639	map of the presheaf-level coequalizer presentation `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`. -/
640	noncomputable def relTensorProj (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
641	    relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q ⟶
642	      (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj
643	        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q) where
644	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
645	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
646	  naturality {U V} f := by
647	    -- NATURALITY (the square `projL_V ∘ domain.map f = apex.map f ∘ projL_U`).  We prove the
648	    -- underlying `ℤ`-linear square as `key` and transport it to the categorical square in `Ab`.
649	    -- An element-level `⊗`-induction at the `Ab` level is blocked by the `AddCommGrpCat.of` carrier
650	    -- instance mismatch (`map_add` fails to fire on the bundled `Ab`-morphism applied to `a + b`);
651	    -- working with bare `ℤ`-linear maps and `TensorProduct.ext'` sidesteps it entirely.  On an
652	    -- elementary tensor `m ⊗ₜ n` both composites send it to
653	    -- `(objRestrict P f m) ⊗ₜ[R(V)] (objRestrict Q f n)` definitionally: the LHS via
654	    -- `TensorProduct.map`+`projL`, the RHS via `projL`+`tensorObj_map_tmul`
655	    -- (both `⊗ₜ`-on-the-nose).  The `S = X.sheaf.obj.obj V` vs `R.obj V` base-ring discrepancy is a
656	    -- `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`-identity, so the elementary-tensor case is `rfl` (no instance
657	    -- re-synthesis, since the existing goal instances are reused).
658	    have key :
659	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (↑(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↑(P.obj V)) (↑(Q.obj V))).comp
660	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f))
661	          = (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom
662	                (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj
663	                  (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q)).map
664	                    f)).toIntLinearMap.comp
665	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (↑(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↑(P.obj U))
666	                (↑(Q.obj U))) := by
667	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
668	      intro m n
669	      rfl
670	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
671	    ext z
672	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
673	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
674	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply, AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap] using hz
675	
676	/-- The cofork condition for the presheaf-level relative-tensor coequalizer: the left- and
677	right-action rows compose equally with the projection, `a_L ≫ π = a_R ≫ π`, as natural
678	transformations of `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`.  Objectwise it is
679	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.coeq_condition`. -/
680	lemma relTensorActL_proj_eq (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
681	    relTensorActL P Q ≫ relTensorProj P Q = relTensorActR P Q ≫ relTensorProj P Q := by
682	  ext U : 2
683	  exact RelativeTensorCoequalizer.coeq_condition (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)
684	
685	/- Planner strategy: 3-step promotion (blueprint `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` proof):
686	1. OBJECTWISE — at each `U`, instantiate `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` with
687	   `S = O_X(U)`, `M = P(U)`, `N = Q(U)`. (API DONE axiom-clean.)
688	2. PROMOTE — the three objectwise families ARE `relTensorActL`/`relTensorActR`/`relTensorProj`
689	   (already natural). A functor-category cocone is a colimit iff every evaluation is, via
690	   `CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` [Mathlib, verify with leansearch].
691	   NOTE (iter-063): leansearch only finds `CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsLimits`
692	   (limits), not the colimit version; the colimit analogue may be
693	   `PresheafOfModules.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` or
694	   `CategoryTheory.Limits.combinedIsColimit` — verify before use.
695	3. APEX — identify the apex presheaf `U ↦ P(U) ⊗_{O_X(U)} Q(U)` with the underlying Ab-presheaf
696	   of `P ⊗_p Q` via `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj` (verified in Mathlib);
697	   transport the colimit along it.
698	Reusable recipe: the `TensorProduct.ext'`→transport-to-`Ab` idiom from `relTensorProj.naturality`
699	is the carrier-bookkeeping pattern. `(P ⊗ Q)` in a fresh `have` must be written
700	`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q` (bare `⊗` re-resolves to TensorProduct).
701	-/
702	/-- The underlying abelian-group presheaf of the presheaf-level relative tensor product
703	`P ⊗_p Q` is the coequalizer, in the functor category `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`, of the parallel pair
704	`relTensorActL P Q` / `relTensorActR P Q` with cofork leg `relTensorProj P Q`.  This is the
705	presheaf-level promotion of `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` (the objectwise content of
706	`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`): colimits in a functor category are computed objectwise, so
707	the objectwise coequalizer at each `U` promotes to a coequalizer in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`.
708	(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`, `lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib`,
709	`lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib`.) -/
710	noncomputable def relativeTensorCoequalizerIso (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
711	    Limits.IsColimit (Limits.Cofork.ofπ (relTensorProj P Q) (relTensorActL_proj_eq P Q)) :=
712	  evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits _ fun U =>
713	    (isColimitMapCoconeCoforkEquiv ((evaluation _ _).obj U) (relTensorActL_proj_eq P Q)).symm
714	      (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U))
715	
716	/-
717	### Action / projection natural transformations of the coequalizer rows — DEFERRED (handoff)
718	
719	The next promotion step assembles `actLmap`/`actRmap`/`projL` into NATURAL transformations of
720	`(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` between `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q`, `relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, and the
721	apex `(toPresheaf).obj (P ⊗_p Q)`, then lifts the cofork via
722	`CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` (apex identified by
723	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj`) to `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`
724	(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).
725
